methods.name: push()
methods.description: Adds one or more elements to the end of an array. Returns the new length.

methods.name: pop()
methods.description: Removes the last element from an array and returns it.

methods.name: shift()
methods.description: Removes the first element from an array and returns it.

methods.name: unshift()
methods.description: Adds one or more elements to the beginning of an array. Returns the new length.

methods.name: splice()
methods.description: Adds or removes elements at a given index. Mutates the original array.

methods.name: slice()
methods.description: Returns a shallow copy of a portion of an array. Does not mutate the original.

methods.name: map()
methods.description: Creates a new array by calling a function on every element.

methods.name: filter()
methods.description: Creates a new array with all elements that pass the test function.

methods.name: reduce()
methods.description: Executes a reducer function on each element, returning a single value.

methods.name: find()
methods.description: Returns the first element that satisfies the test. Returns undefined if not found.

methods.name: findIndex()
methods.description: Returns the index of the first element that satisfies the test. Returns -1 if not found.

methods.name: indexOf()
methods.description: Returns the first index of the given element. Returns -1 if not found.

methods.name: includes()
methods.description: Returns true if the array contains the given value, false otherwise.

methods.name: some()
methods.description: Returns true if at least one element passes the test.

methods.name: every()
methods.description: Returns true only if all elements pass the test.

methods.name: flat()
methods.description: Creates a new array with sub-array elements concatenated up to a given depth.

methods.name: flatMap()
methods.description: Maps each element then flattens the result by one level.

methods.name: forEach()
methods.description: Executes a function once for each element. Returns undefined.

methods.name: sort()
methods.description: Sorts elements in place. Without a comparator, converts elements to strings.

methods.name: reverse()
methods.description: Reverses the array in place and returns it.

methods.name: concat()
methods.description: Returns a new array by merging two or more arrays. Does not mutate.

methods.name: join()
methods.description: Joins all elements into a string, separated by the given separator.

methods.name: fill()
methods.description: Fills elements with a static value from start to end index. Mutates the array.

methods.name: Array.from()
methods.description: Creates a new array from an array-like or iterable object.

methods.name: Array.isArray()
methods.description: Returns true if the value is an array.

methods.name: Array.of()
methods.description: Creates a new array from the given arguments.

methods.name: length
methods.description: Property that returns the number of characters in a string.

methods.name: toUpperCase()
methods.description: Returns the string converted to uppercase.

methods.name: toLowerCase()
methods.description: Returns the string converted to lowercase.

methods.name: trim()
methods.description: Removes whitespace from both ends of a string.

methods.name: trimStart()
methods.description: Removes whitespace from the beginning of a string.

methods.name: trimEnd()
methods.description: Removes whitespace from the end of a string.

methods.name: slice()
methods.description: Extracts a section of a string and returns it as a new string.

methods.name: substring()
methods.description: Returns the part of a string between start and end indexes.

methods.name: split()
methods.description: Splits a string into an array of substrings.

methods.name: replace()
methods.description: Replaces the first occurrence of a substring or regex match.

methods.name: replaceAll()
methods.description: Replaces all occurrences of a substring.

methods.name: includes()
methods.description: Returns true if the string contains the given substring.

methods.name: startsWith()
methods.description: Returns true if the string starts with the given substring.

methods.name: endsWith()
methods.description: Returns true if the string ends with the given substring.

methods.name: indexOf()
methods.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of a substring. Returns -1 if not found.

methods.name: lastIndexOf()
methods.description: Returns the index of the last occurrence of a substring.

methods.name: padStart()
methods.description: Pads the beginning of a string with another string until the target length is reached.

methods.name: padEnd()
methods.description: Pads the end of a string with another string until the target length is reached.

methods.name: repeat()
methods.description: Returns a new string with the original repeated a given number of times.

methods.name: charAt()
methods.description: Returns the character at the specified index.

methods.name: charCodeAt()
methods.description: Returns the UTF-16 code unit value of the character at the given index.

methods.name: at()
methods.description: Returns the character at the given index. Supports negative indexing from the end.

methods.name: match()
methods.description: Returns an array of matches against a regular expression.

methods.name: matchAll()
methods.description: Returns an iterator of all regex matches including capture groups.

methods.name: String.fromCharCode()
methods.description: Returns a string created from the given UTF-16 code unit values.

methods.name: Object.keys()
methods.description: Returns an array of the object's own enumerable property names.

methods.name: Object.values()
methods.description: Returns an array of the object's own enumerable property values.

methods.name: Object.entries()
methods.description: Returns an array of [key, value] pairs for each own enumerable property.

methods.name: Object.assign()
methods.description: Copies own enumerable properties from source objects into the target. Returns the target.
methods.example:
Object.assign({ a: 1 }, { b: 2 });
// returns { a: 1, b: 2 }

methods.name: Object.freeze()
methods.description: Prevents new properties from being added and existing ones from being changed.

methods.name: Object.isFrozen()
methods.description: Returns true if the object is frozen.

methods.name: Object.create()
methods.description: Creates a new object with the specified prototype.

methods.name: Object.defineProperty()
methods.description: Adds or modifies a property on an object with fine-grained control.

methods.name: Object.getOwnPropertyNames()
methods.description: Returns all own property names including non-enumerable ones.

methods.name: Object.getPrototypeOf()
methods.description: Returns the prototype of the given object.

methods.name: Object.fromEntries()
methods.description: Creates an object from an array of [key, value] pairs. The inverse of Object.entries().

methods.name: Object.hasOwn()
methods.description: Returns true if the object has the given property as its own (not inherited).

methods.name: hasOwnProperty()
methods.description: Returns true if the object has the given property as a direct property.

methods.name: Object.seal()
methods.description: Prevents adding or removing properties but allows modifying existing ones.

methods.name: Object.is()
methods.description: Determines whether two values are the same. Handles NaN and -0 correctly.

methods.name: Function Declaration
methods.description: Defines a named function that is hoisted to the top of its scope.

methods.name: Function Expression
methods.description: Assigns an anonymous function to a variable. Not hoisted.

methods.name: Arrow Function
methods.description: Concise syntax for functions. Does not have its own `this`, `arguments`, or `prototype`.

methods.name: Default Parameters
methods.description: Allows parameters to have default values if not provided or undefined.

methods.name: Rest Parameters
methods.description: Collects all remaining arguments into a real array.

methods.name: call()
methods.description: Calls a function with a given `this` value and individual arguments.

methods.name: apply()
methods.description: Calls a function with a given `this` and arguments provided as an array.

methods.name: bind()
methods.description: Returns a new function permanently bound to a given `this` and optional arguments.

methods.name: IIFE
methods.description: Immediately Invoked Function Expression — executes right after being defined.

methods.name: Closure
methods.description: A function that remembers the variables of its outer scope even after the outer function returns.

methods.name: Generator Function
methods.description: A function that can pause and resume execution using yield.

methods.name: toString()
methods.description: Returns the source code of the function as a string.

methods.name: typeof
methods.description: Returns a string indicating the type of the operand.

methods.name: instanceof
methods.description: Returns true if the value was created from the given constructor.

methods.name: Number
methods.description: The number type represents both integers and floating-point values. Special values: Infinity, -Infinity, NaN.

methods.name: String
methods.description: A sequence of characters. Can be created with quotes or the String() constructor.

methods.name: Boolean
methods.description: Has only two values: true or false. Falsy values: false, 0, '', null, undefined, NaN.

methods.name: null
methods.description: Represents the intentional absence of a value. typeof null returns 'object' — a historical bug.

methods.name: undefined
methods.description: A variable that has been declared but not assigned has the value undefined.

methods.name: BigInt
methods.description: Represents integers larger than Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (2^53 - 1).

methods.name: Symbol
methods.description: A unique and immutable primitive value, often used as object property keys.

methods.name: NaN
methods.description: Not-a-Number. Result of invalid numeric operations. NaN !== NaN is always true.

methods.name: Type Coercion
methods.description: JS automatically converts types when using == or arithmetic. Use === to avoid coercion.

methods.name: Number.isInteger()
methods.description: Returns true if the value is an integer.

methods.name: Number.isFinite()
methods.description: Returns true if the value is a finite number.

methods.name: Number.parseInt()
methods.description: Parses a string and returns an integer in the specified radix.

methods.name: Number.parseFloat()
methods.description: Parses a string and returns a floating-point number.

methods.name: Primitive Types
methods.description: TypeScript supports string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, bigint as primitive types.

methods.name: Type Alias
methods.description: Creates a reusable name for any type including unions, intersections, and complex types.

methods.name: Interface
methods.description: Describes the shape of an object. Interfaces can be extended and merged.

methods.name: Union Type
methods.description: A value that can be one of several types.

methods.name: Intersection Type
methods.description: Combines multiple types into one. The value must satisfy all types.

methods.name: Generics
methods.description: Allows creating reusable components that work with a variety of types.

methods.name: Enum
methods.description: A set of named constants. Can be numeric or string-based.

methods.name: Tuple
methods.description: An array with a fixed number of elements with known types at each position.

methods.name: Partial<T>
methods.description: Makes all properties of a type optional.

methods.name: Required<T>
methods.description: Makes all optional properties of a type required.

methods.name: Readonly<T>
methods.description: Makes all properties of a type read-only.

methods.name: Pick<T, K>
methods.description: Constructs a type with only the specified properties.
methods.example:
type User = { id: number; name: string; age: number };
type Preview = Pick<User, 'id' | 'name'>;

methods.name: Omit<T, K>
methods.description: Constructs a type by removing specified properties.
methods.example:
type User = { id: number; name: string; age: number };
type NoAge = Omit<User, 'age'>;

methods.name: Record<K, V>
methods.description: Constructs a type with a set of keys mapped to a value type.
methods.example:
type Roles = Record<string, 'admin' | 'user'>;
const roles: Roles = { alice: 'admin', bob: 'user' };

methods.name: ReturnType<T>
methods.description: Extracts the return type of a function type.
methods.example:
function getUser() { return { name: 'Alex', age: 25 }; }
type User = ReturnType<typeof getUser>;

methods.name: as const
methods.description: Tells TypeScript to infer the narrowest possible type, making values readonly literals.
methods.example:
const config = { env: 'production' } as const;
// config.env: 'production' not string

methods.name: keyof
methods.description: Produces a union type of all keys of a given type.

methods.name: typeof (type context)
methods.description: In a type context, extracts the TypeScript type of a variable.

methods.name: Math.round()
methods.description: Returns the value of a number rounded to the nearest integer.

methods.name: Math.floor()
methods.description: Returns the largest integer less than or equal to x.

methods.name: Math.ceil()
methods.description: Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to x.

methods.name: Math.max()
methods.description: Returns the largest of the given numbers.

methods.name: Math.min()
methods.description: Returns the smallest of the given numbers.

methods.name: Math.abs()
methods.description: Returns the absolute value of a number.

methods.name: Math.sqrt()
methods.description: Returns the square root of a number.

methods.name: Math.pow()
methods.description: Returns base raised to the power of exponent. Same as ** operator.

methods.name: Math.random()
methods.description: Returns a pseudo-random floating-point number between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive).

methods.name: Math.trunc()
methods.description: Returns the integer part of a number by removing the fractional digits.

methods.name: Date.now()
methods.description: Returns the current timestamp in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

methods.name: new Date()
methods.description: Creates a Date object representing a point in time.

methods.name: toFixed()
methods.description: Formats a number to a fixed number of decimal places. Returns a string.

methods.name: toString() (Number)
methods.description: Converts a number to a string in the given radix (base).

methods.name: JSON.stringify()
methods.description: Converts a JavaScript value to a JSON string.

methods.name: JSON.parse()
methods.description: Parses a JSON string and returns the corresponding JavaScript value.

methods.name: console.log()
methods.description: Outputs a message to the web console.

methods.name: setTimeout()
methods.description: Executes a function after a specified delay in milliseconds.

methods.name: setInterval()
methods.description: Repeatedly executes a function with a fixed time delay between each call.

methods.name: Basic Callback
methods.description: A callback is a function passed as an argument and called later by the receiving function.

methods.name: Error-First Callback
methods.description: Node.js convention: the first argument is an error (null if none), the second is the result.

methods.name: setTimeout Callback
methods.description: Defers execution of a callback by a given number of milliseconds.

methods.name: Array Callbacks
methods.description: Array methods like map, filter, forEach, reduce all accept callbacks.

methods.name: Event Listener Callback
methods.description: Attaches a callback that fires when a DOM event occurs.

methods.name: Callback Hell
methods.description: Deeply nested callbacks that are hard to read. Solved by Promises or async/await.

methods.name: Synchronous Callback
methods.description: A callback executed immediately in the current call stack, not deferred.

methods.name: Asynchronous Callback
methods.description: A callback placed in the event queue and run after the current call stack clears.

methods.name: Arithmetic Operators
methods.description: Perform basic math: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, modulo, exponentiation.

methods.name: Assignment Operators
methods.description: Assign values. Compound variants combine an operation with assignment.

methods.name: Comparison Operators
methods.description: Compare two values. === and !== check value AND type (strict).

methods.name: Logical Operators
methods.description: && returns first falsy or last value. || returns first truthy or last value. ! negates.

methods.name: Nullish Coalescing (??)
methods.description: Returns the right-hand side only if the left is null or undefined. Unlike ||, it ignores false and 0.

methods.name: Optional Chaining (?.)
methods.description: Safely access deeply nested properties. Returns undefined instead of throwing if a part is null.

methods.name: Ternary Operator
methods.description: A concise one-line if/else expression.

methods.name: Logical Assignment (&&=, ||=, ??=)
methods.description: Assigns the right value only when the logical condition holds.

methods.name: Spread Operator (...)
methods.description: Expands an iterable (array/object) into individual elements.

methods.name: Destructuring Assignment
methods.syntax: const { a, b } = obj  |  const [x, y] = arr
methods.description: Extracts values from arrays or objects into distinct variables.
methods.example:
const { name, age } = { name: 'Alex', age: 25 };
const [first, second] = [10, 20];

methods.name: Bitwise Operators
methods.description: Operate on 32-bit binary representations of numbers.

methods.name: in Operator
methods.description: Returns true if the specified property exists in the object or its prototype chain.

methods.name: delete Operator
methods.description: Removes a property from an object. Returns true if successful.

methods.name: void Operator
methods.description: Evaluates an expression and returns undefined.

methods.name: if / else
methods.description: Executes a block of code if the condition is truthy, otherwise executes the else block.

methods.name: else if
methods.description: Chains multiple conditions. The first truthy branch executes.

methods.name: switch / case
methods.description: Compares a value against multiple cases using strict equality. Use break to prevent fall-through.

methods.name: Ternary Operator
methods.description: Inline conditional. Returns a if truthy, b if falsy.

methods.name: Short-circuit &&
methods.description: If condition is falsy, expression is not evaluated. Returns first falsy or last value.

methods.name: Short-circuit ||
methods.description: Returns the first truthy value. Commonly used for defaults.

methods.name: Nullish Coalescing ??
methods.description: Like ||, but only falls back for null/undefined, not for 0 or ''.

methods.name: Truthy & Falsy
methods.description: Falsy values: false, 0, 0n, '', null, undefined, NaN. Everything else is truthy.

methods.name: Optional Chaining in Conditions
methods.description: Safely check nested properties without risking a TypeError.

methods.name: Promise
methods.description: Represents a value that will be available in the future. States: pending, fulfilled, rejected.

methods.name: .then()
methods.description: Attaches a handler for when the promise resolves. Returns a new promise.

methods.name: .catch()
methods.description: Handles promise rejections. Equivalent to .then(null, onRejected).

methods.name: .finally()
methods.description: Runs a callback when the promise settles regardless of outcome.

methods.name: async / await
methods.description: Syntactic sugar over Promises. async makes a function return a Promise. await pauses execution.

methods.name: try / catch with async
methods.description: Handles errors in async/await code using standard try/catch syntax.

methods.name: Promise.all()
methods.description: Waits for ALL promises to resolve. Rejects immediately if any promise rejects.

methods.name: Promise.allSettled()
methods.description: Waits for ALL promises to settle (resolve or reject). Never rejects.

methods.name: Promise.race()
methods.description: Returns the result of the first promise to settle (resolve or reject).

methods.name: Promise.any()
methods.description: Resolves with the first promise that fulfills. Rejects only if all promises reject.

methods.name: Promise.resolve()
methods.description: Returns a promise that is already resolved with the given value.

methods.name: Event Loop
methods.description: JS is single-threaded. The event loop processes: call stack first, then microtasks (Promises), then macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval).

methods.name: Map
methods.description: A collection of key-value pairs where keys can be any type. Preserves insertion order.

methods.name: Map.set()
methods.description: Adds or updates an entry in the Map. Returns the Map itself.

methods.name: Map.get()
methods.description: Returns the value for the given key, or undefined if not found.

methods.name: Map.has()
methods.description: Returns true if the Map contains the given key.

methods.name: Map.delete()
methods.description: Removes the entry with the given key. Returns true if it existed.

methods.name: Set
methods.description: A collection of unique values of any type. Duplicates are automatically ignored.

methods.name: Set.add()
methods.description: Adds a value to the Set. Has no effect if the value already exists.

methods.name: Set.has()
methods.description: Returns true if the value exists in the Set.

methods.name: Set.delete()
methods.description: Removes a value from the Set. Returns true if it existed.

methods.name: WeakMap
methods.description: Like Map but keys must be objects. Keys are weakly held — garbage collected when no other refs exist.

methods.name: WeakSet
methods.description: Like Set but only holds objects. Entries are weakly held and not enumerable.

methods.name: Stack (using Array)
methods.description: LIFO (Last In First Out) structure. Use push to add and pop to remove from the top.

methods.name: Queue (using Array)
methods.description: FIFO (First In First Out) structure. Use push to enqueue and shift to dequeue.

methods.name: Linked List Node
methods.description: A chain of nodes where each node holds a value and a reference to the next node.

methods.name: Deduplicate with Set
methods.description: A common pattern to remove duplicates from an array using Set.
